title: alnus incana var. serrulata
intro: Alnus incana var. serrulata, also known as the mountain alder, is a variety of alder tree that is native to North America, characterized by its serrated leaves and ability to thrive in wet environments. The mountain alder has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent qualities. The health benefits of the mountain alder include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating wounds, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Mountain alder can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and salves, allowing for a range of medicinal applications.
